reviews.review_description: this looks great. my quistion is:  will the apps that says oculus go work with the gear vr ? theres probley a lot of gear vr owners that would like to know as well.thank you.

developer_response.body: Yes! Looking Glass VR will work on Gear VR with or without the controller accessory.

reviews.review_title: Cool little old timey viewfinder dealie.
reviews.review_description: I swear I played this before, but I guess not?
Like others said, it 'd be great to use our own music.
I enjoyed learning more while mersed in this environment

I love the room this is set in. Honestly the environment is my favorite part.  I could just sit and look at the fire and listen to the victrola. If it would be possible to change the music to audio files on my device I'd happily spend hours in this soon listening to audio books.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review and suggestion - I will endeavor to include this feature one day soon!

reviews.review_description: I'm glad this exists!
Black and white historical pictures with 3D  depth. The educational text accompanying each photo is a little to small to read comfortably.

developer_response.body: You're not wrong - reading in VR is tough at the best of times. I hope to provide audio narration of the card copy one day, but until then I can suggest swiping backwards on your touch pad to bring the cards a little closer. Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: One of the best vr apps I've ever used. Brilliantly created.
I love it...just wish there was more
Nice little viewer app. Great pics, and the record player was a nice touch. The clock ticking was a little loud and annoying, but at least it kept time.

reviews.review_title: I haven't looked yet but know it's great
reviews.review_description: What a great idea!  Travelling salesmen used to come to your door with stereo-card collections, you could buy:  LONDON, or GRAND CANYON and 'virtually' explore those places!  They show one of the viewers in the movie 'Voyage to the Center of the Earth' (the one with Brendan Frasier) he take a nit out of the package from hos Brother early in the film, A Stereo-Opticon!  I remember 2 past lives and we used these like TV back in those days before TV!

I loved this app on the Go, but where’s the Quest port?!? 

It’s wild to me that these photos have survived a hundred years, but the app survived only a few! Goes to show the value of physical prints!
Immersive old style 3D experience.
Virtual environment for 100+ stereoscopic photographs.

reviews.review_title: And your thought 3D pictures were new!
reviews.review_description: This is a great app for stereoscopy lovers! You have 176 "vintage" stereo pictures to browse, including their original descriptions on the back. My main complaint is that the images should be color corrected in order to remove the yellow tinting. The app runs fine on a Note 4.
Nice one - for a freebie. If the app had the functionality to teleport and sit and chill out in front of the fire, I would give it a full 5 stars.

reviews.review_title: When will you adapt this to Quest?
reviews.review_description: I liked it but I dont have a Go anymore. I don't want to think I wasted money buying something I can't use.
The room has very nice graphics. Text is too small to be readable. Are all photographs genuine stereoscopic shots ? Some depths look artificial...

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review! Yes, the stereo effect in each photo is entirely from the original cards. I have noticed that"paper cut out" look too, and looked into it: In William C Darrah's book "The World of Stereographs" he explains that some stereophotographers deliberately exaggerated the stereo effect - it even has a name: "hyperspace".

Regarding text size, I hope to provide audio narration of the card copy one day, but until then I can suggest swiping backwards on your touch pad to bring the cards a little closer. Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: Very intersting, simple to handle ..many more features can be added
I love everything about this. My only suggestion would be to allow choosing a new collection from within the room.

developer_response.body: We agree completely!  This is in the works but some other, deeper changes need to be made first.  Stay tuned...
